generated_parts.label: Service Port + Flex-Wiring Inspection Cover
generated_parts.spec: Flex_wiring closure module: service_loop inspection cover, strain_relief, bend_radius_note, connector keying, charge/debug service port, power vs signal separation label

requirements: Knuckle-crossing wiring with service loops and flex-rated bend radius
Good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
Good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
Good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.
Good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
Good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
Good: Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access.
Good: Flexion-resistance actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row.
Good: Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance.
Good: Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles.
Good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry.
Good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals.
Good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns.

Good: Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type (bolted, threaded_insert, snap_fit, press_fit, dovetail_slide, clevis_pin, revolute_bearing, band_clamp, adhesive_lap, o_ring, connector_mate, harness_passthrough) with its DOF and a numeric clearance.

Good: Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable; no installed part blocks access to a later one; last-installed and captive parts are identified.
Good: One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme, so tolerance does not accumulate uncontrolled across the stack.
Good: Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts and high-load joints use load-appropriate connection types.

Good: Compared to the previous version the design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints.
Good: The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture; each remaining interface is justified by service, motion, manufacture split, or load transfer.

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be within ±10% of the design budget.

verify.value: All required evidence_features are present in part_tree/geometry_ops.
verify.rule: Each required evidence_feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.

verify.value: All interfaces are reciprocal with neighboring subsystems.
verify.rule: Each interface must have a matching reciprocal declaration in the neighboring subsystem.

verify.value: Visible locking evidence is present for all critical joints.
verify.rule: Critical joints must show visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential strain on flex wiring due to insufficient strain relief.
risk.mit: Increase strain relief geometry around flex wiring exits.

risk.desc: Risk of mis-mating connectors due to lack of clear keying.
risk.mit: Add distinct keying features to all connectors.

risk.desc: Insufficient access for maintenance and inspection of internal components.
risk.mit: Design additional access points or larger inspection covers.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal issues if strain relief does not manage heat properly.
risk.mit: Evaluate strain relief design for thermal management and adjust if necessary.

risk.desc: Risk of incorrect assembly sequence leading to blocked access.
risk.mit: Clarify assembly sequence and ensure all parts are accessible during installation.

Thermal management through strain relief geometry.

Provide clear visual access for inspection and maintenance.

Check the strength and durability of all interfaces under expected loads.
